comments.comment_text:
a) It is important to ensure all personnel onboard do not work in excess of 12 hours per day, and sufficient rest per legal requirements. Please can you confirm Geoview are compliant with legal requirements. 
It appears that based on Figure 4, the marine crew have a 6 hour shift. Is this a practice in South Korea? How can Geoview ensure personnel have sufficient unrestricted rest per legal requirements?
It appears that project crew have a 4 hour shifts. which means that each group only has 8 hours of rest before they start their shift? is this correct? Does this comply with legal requirements and ensure not work more than 12 hours a day?

XDHUY - 15/01/2025
Good to hear you are in compliance with Korean Law. 
With this being said, it still looks like personnel (survey and marine crew) do not have 12 hours of non-disturbed rest with 6 hours rest and 8 hours. Understood that in total they get more than 12 hours I am concerned whether this is sufficient rest each time. Are the crew comfortable with this approach and how long has this been used for? If this is the practice in Geoview and crew are comfortable, then this is fine. 
In regards to frequent entabglement, only the crew onshift need to respond. If it extends beyond their shift than they will get the same rest.

[Follow-up]
b) What is the chef's shift schedule? Is there an alternative method in case an issue arise with the chef?
comments.response_text:
a) We essentially operate on an 8-hour workday in compliance with Korea Labor Law. Including weekends, this is total 56 hours. Therefore, we implement a "flexible working" schedule that allows up to 56 hours under the law, but any excessive work hours are trying to be adjusted to a maximum of 44 hours through compensatory time.
Additionally, if we operate day/night shifts, we can't ensure 12 hours of rest for surveyors due to frequent entanglements. Therefore, we operate 3 shifts to provide 16 hours of rest. This ensures at least 12 hours of rest per day based on Geoview's experience on previous projects.
We are seeking a method for continuous 12 hours of rest through an agreement between labor and management.

Geoview has been implementing this approach since 2021, following the introduction of 24-hour operations. After evaluating and experimenting with several methods, this working arrangement under agree with all geoview members was adopted as the most effective and suitable solution.
comments.summary_ko: 1일 12시간 초과 근무 금지 및 법적 휴식 요건 준수 확인 필요.

comments.comment_text:
a) Please clarify why QC team only work from 08:00 to 18:00. 
Is the vessel not working 24 hours?

Further in the document (section 3), it mentions only 2 people conducting QC. Is this sufficient for 24 hours operations?
comments.response_text:
a) The method is different. We transit the acquisition data on previous day(00:00~24:00) to sever every 6am and QC guy semi-porocess the data and submit the QC report at 4pm.
And QC computers will work for data coverting during night.
Surveyor is checking data quality on real time and ask quiality to QC team whenever find any symptoms.
comments.summary_ko: QC팀 08:00-18:00 근무 사유 설명 필요. 24시간 운항 여부 확인. QC 인원 2명 충분성 검토.

comments.comment_text:
a) "The Client is responsible for overall SIMOPS management between any asset working in
the development area." - Orsted do not have overall responsibilty. Orsted will of course advise and assist in communication especially with other vessels contracted by Orsted. [Follow-up]
b)
Perhaps, I do not understand this "Is the fishing boats who has agreement with Orste included to cotractor? ". Any vessels in area, Geoview should follow colregs and safely & peacefuly work around. This involves contacting any fishing boats on site. 
The base plan is to always work around the fishing equipment and fisherman. We should not create any conflict.Any communication with fisherman must be done in a respective manner and we should never demand them to stop their fishing/move away, but to explain that we have limited manouvourability and can be dangerous. If there is any confict arising please just sail away and advise on the vessel details to Orsted. 

Hopefully this is clear. If not, we can discuss further. No action is needed on the document and if clear, comment can be closed.

comments.section: 4.4. Management of Guard Vessel Operations, 16 of 62
comments.comment_text:
a) 'Cross-check weather conditions using reliable sources such as KMA and Windy.
Operations will be postponed or canceled if wave heights exceed 1.5 m to ensure the
accuracy of research data, and safety precautions will be taken for conditions above 3 m.'
- Can you confirm which weather data source you'll use for assessing if the survey vessel or the guard vessel can go offshore? 
- How can you supply the weather data records from public weather sources (e.g. KMA, Windy)? 
- Can you clarify guard vessel's operation weather limits, significant wave height (Hs) below 1.5m?

[Follow-up]
b) Received the operation limits to the latest proposed guard vessels (77 Samyoung & Corol No.2), both vessels have weather limit of wave hight (Hs) of 1.5m.
From latest discussion, Geoview will secure other recordable weather report source such as StormGeo and provide the weather reports frequently to Orsted.

The comment can be closed since things been addressed in our other discussion - now we're just waiting for Geoview to inform when we/Orsted can start to receive the weather reports.
c) Geoview mentioned we will receive from 5th Feb in our weekly meeting, is that still the plan? Note that it will be better to issue few days befoew the 1st sail out.
Comment closed and will follow this up with Geoview in vessel progress calls.
comments.response_text:
a) Vessel limitation wias already submiited (Wave/wind/visibility) and we check weather condition at least 2 time every day, and submit weather information for 1 weeks a head through DPR.
Also Geoveiw decides "stop work" or "go to refuge port or area" under special weather report from KMA to follow marine law.
comments.summary_ko: 기상 조건 교차 확인 관련. 파고 1.5m 초과 시 운항 중단 조건과 장비별 제한 조건 구분 필요.

comments.comment_text:
a) Grab samples are not requested by Orsted and not included in the signed contract, can Geoview clarify the followings:
- What's the purpose of the proposed grab samples and if it's needed? Note that Geoview shall obtain approval from Orsted until being allowed to do anything that is not included in scope of signed contract.
- Will the operational time be on Geoview?
- How to treat the HSE requirements and Offshore Supervisor in such out-of-scope operation?

[Follow-up]
b) To clarify, grab sample is not required/requested by Orsted for the project and NOT a 'must to do' to enable Geoview to meet Orsted's requirements. In App 6 section 8.3.3., it's just mentioning items to be included in the Weekly Progress Reporting, but those items will be subject to what items we have  included in the signed contract App 14. But if Geoview thinks it's practical to do so, please let Orsted know and get Orsted's approval on doing so.

Just clarificaion comment and the comment can be closed if it's understood by Geoview.
comments.response_text:
a) Purpose: The purpose of the proposed grab samples is to enhance the identification of seabed sediments compared to the data of MBES and SSS. Direct visual analysis of the collected samples significantly improves the reliability of seabed sediment classification.
It is one of Orsted requiement 8.3.3. on Appendix 6 Scope of work.

Approval and Operation: Geoview will proceed with the operation only if approval is obtained from Orsted. If it is not approved, the operation will not be conducted.
HSE:In the event that the operation is approved, all relevant HSE requirements will be strictly followed, and the CR will be appropriately informed and involved to ensure compliance with safety protocols.(the operating proces is same with tidal gauge)
comments.summary_ko: 그랩 샘플링은 Orsted 미요청·계약 미포함 사항. 목적 및 필요성 설명. 추가 비용·일정 영향 확인.

comments.response_text: a) Please refer to the attached image on the right.

comments.comment_text: a) 'sub-seabed' interpretation is not required from Geoview. You are welcome to use it to support seabed interpretation, but nothing below the seabed should be interpreted. Orsted are doing all interpretation from SBP and M-UHRS.
comments.response_text: a) Noted.delete them on PEP

comments.comment_text:
a) The length between SSS and magnetomter seems excessive. Can you explain the reasons. Can this be shorter?

[Follow-up]
b) To be further discussed in the VKOM (5th Feb), suggesting Geoview can do test during mob to optimise the equipment operation set up.
Comment closed and will follow this up with Geoview in vessel progress calls.
comments.response_text:
a) The cable length between the SSS (Side-Scan Sonar) and the magnetometer has been carefully determined based on the operational altitude requirements of each instrument and to ensure the optimal quality of data acquisition. The distance between the two devices is set so that both can operate stably at their required altitudes while the survey vessel maintains a constant speed.

Although it is technically possible to shorten the distance, doing so may compromise the ability to meet the altitude requirements, which could negatively affect the performance of the SSS and the quality of the magnetometer data. Additionally, reducing the distance could increase the likelihood of magnetic interference from the acoustic signals or electronic equipment of the SSS, which might distort the magnetometer’s measurements, and it is manufacture company're recommendation.
comments.summary_ko: SSS-자력계 간 예인 거리 과도. 단축 가능 여부 및 사유 설명. VKOM에서 추가 논의 예정.

comments.comment_text: a) Raw Deliverables are only to be provided upon request. Please do not send this to us, only if we ask.
comments.response_text: a) Updated in PEP 14.3. Raw deliverables will only be provided upon the client’s request.
comments.summary_ko: Raw Deliverables는 요청 시에만 제공. 자동 전송 금지.

comments.section:
1.2 Scope of Work & 4.1. Specifications of the Survey Vessel
GEOVIEW, 11/17 of 73
comments.comment_text:
a) 
i. Can you specify this PEP document will be specifically used for Lot 2 scope (the to-be-VOed scopes, i.e. Lot 1 and Lot 3, will have separate PEPs)? 
ii. 'GEOVIEW plans to deploy GEOVIEW DP-1 for this project, which will be used to conduct
MBES, SSS, MAG, and SBP surveys', similar comment, can you change the wording from '…for this project...' to '...for Lot 2 scope...'? 

Clarification comment and the comment can be closed if it's clear for Geoview that this PEP will be used for Lot 2 scope only, the VO scopes , i.e. Lot 1 and Lot 3, will have separate PEPs.
comments.response_text: a) It has been updated in PEP(Section1.2, 4.1)
comments.summary_ko: PEP 문서가 Lot 2 전용임을 명시. VO 범위(Lot 1, 3)는 별도 PEP. DP-1 배치 관련 상세 내용 추가.

comments.comment_text:
a) 'Port calls will be planned to minimize any impact on the project schedule. These visits will
be primarily for crew changes, resupply, and equipment checks. Ports will be selected based
on their accessibility and the availability of required services.'
i. Can you specify what the mentioned 'equipment checks' refer to, that will require a portcall? Note that if it's due to equipment mulfunction that will need to be clarified if the time to be categorised as 'Standby Other – Contractor’s account (equipment downtime)' or 'Planned/Unplanned Portcall' in the DPR. 
ii. Please add your provided comment reply in comment #3 here - 'The primary port is Incheon north port but can use Pyeongtaek port as backup when we can't arrange primary port.', to specify the port options for the portcall. 
iii. Can you also indicate transit time difference with using Incheon North Port or Pyeongtaek Port?

Clarification comment and the comment can be closed if Geoview can provide written/verbal reply to above questions.
comments.response_text:
a)
i. The 'equipment checks' refer to the inspection and verification of equipment status and functionality after a port call to ensure readiness for the next survey operation. "equipment checks" was deleted to avoid misleading. 
If the equipment check is due to a malfunction, this will be categorized as either 'Standby Other – Contractor’s account (equipment downtime)' in the DPR.
ii. It has been updated in PEP.
iii) The distance from Incheon North Port to Lot 2 is approximately 54 km, taking into account the navigation route. Assuming a vessel speed of 10 knots, the estimated travel time is about 3 hours.
The distance from Pyeongtaek Port to Lot 2 is approximately 75 km, considering the navigation route. Assuming a vessel speed of 10 knots, the estimated travel time is about 4 hours.
comments.summary_ko: 항구 기항 관련 사전 Orsted 승인 절차 추가. 기항 시 Client Representative 하선 관련 사항 기재.

comments.section: 4.4. Management of Guard Vessel Operations, 18/19 of 73
comments.comment_text:
a) 
i. 'Detect and respond to the presence of fishing nets and other obstacles in the survey
area.', please be noted that no matter guard vessel or Geoview's survey vessel should prevent interactions with/ damage to the onsite fishing gears unless emergent situations and for safety reasons, and Geoview should keep records of (if any) interactions, e.g. entanglements, with onsite fishing gears. Orsted will provide a template for that soon. 
ii. 'Operations will be postponed or canceled if wave heights exceed 1.5 m to ensure the
accuracy of research data, and safety precautions will be taken for conditions above 3 m.', suggest to change the wording 'research data' to 'scout data' (to prevent the 'research' wording triggers a MSRA permit to enable the guard vessel works).
iii. 'Establish hourly communication with the survey vessel via VHF to update position and
status.', I assume this will not limit the immediate/ad hoc communications between the guard vessel and survey vessel, whenever guard vessel sees any unsafe situations (e.g. drifting nets, approaching vessels, etc.) they will inform the survey vessel immediately.

Clarification comment and the comment can be closed if above is all clear and also consistent with Geoview's plan.
comments.response_text:
a) i. Noted.
ii. It has been updated in PEP.
iii. Noted.
comments.summary_ko: 어망 등 장애물 대응 시 어민과의 상호작용 방지 필수. Guard vessel 전시 주의사항 기재.

comments.section: Table 9. Survey Equipment & Table 10. Survey Spare Equipment, 22/23 of 73
comments.comment_text:
a) As Table 9 & Table 10 shows:
i. Can Geoview clarify if below is correct understanding for number of of equipment 'onboard' the vessel?
- GNSS: primary (Hemisphere R632 GNSS Receiver) 1x + secondary (Hemisphere R330 GNSS Receiver) 1x 
- USBL: main (Sonardyne Mini-Ranger-2) 1x + back up/spare (Sonardyne Mini-Ranger) 2x
- MRU: main (iXblue Octans Surface) 1x + no back up/spare
- SVP: main (AML-3) 1x + back up/spare (AML-3) 1x
- MBES transducer/receiver: main (Teledyne T50-ER) 1x + no back up/spare
- SBP transducer/receiver: main (Innomar Deep 36) 1x + no back up/spare
- SSS towfish: main (Edgetech 4205 - 120/410 kHz) 1x + back up/spare (Edgetech 4205 - 120/410 kHz) 1x
- MAG: main (Geometrics G-882 Magnetometer) 1x + no back up/spare
- Grab sampler 1x (not required by Orsted)
ii. There seems to be no spare MAG onboard, does Geoview think it will cause any problems since the high entanglement risk in this project site?

[Follow-up]
b) Clarified in the VKOM (5th Feb) - Geoview will bring necessary back up equipment onboard, including USBL, SVP, SSS, MAG, etc.
comments.response_text:
a) The most of back up equipment is on Geoview warehouse to provide to any project within 24hours.
EM-712 is mounted on vessel.
2ea USBL beacons will be on board as backup.
Please refer updated information.
GEOVIEW acknowledge the potential risk of entanglement at the project site. To mitigate this, an additional MAG unit is scheduled to be loaded onboard by mid-February.

comments.comment_text:
a) 'The surveyors will identify quality issues and determine any lines requiring re-acquisition, reporting these findings to the PM.', can you clarify the responsibility of Geoview's personnel, Project Manager v.s. Party Chief? And how the communication/decision will be made if any rerun identified due to the data quality issue, Orsted Offshoer Supervisor/Client Represensative will need to be informed and give the go ahead of reruns? 

Clarification comment and the comment can be closed if Geoview can provide written/verbal reply to above questions.

[Follow-up]
b) Clarified in the VKOM (5th Feb).
comments.response_text:
a) The Party Chief oversees daily survey operations and is responsible for identifying data issues and proposing lines for re-acquisition. The PM holds overall responsibility for the project and makes final decisions on operational adjustments, including re-acquisition, in consultation with the client.
If data quality issues require re-acquisition, the Surveyor, QC Team, and Party Chief will collaborate to review the situation. The Party Chief will then communicate with the PM, who will inform the Client Representative (CR) and seek approval for the re-acquisition.
comments.summary_ko: Geoview 인원 역할 명확화. PM, Lead Surveyor, Onshore QC 간 책임 구분 및 보고 체계.

comments.comment_text:
a) 
i. Low temparature risk: will Geoview have tools/methodology to deal with potential frozen stuffs on back deck to prevent downtime of the operation?
ii. There's no mention of entanglement risk/ fishing gears risk, suggest to add this high risk if not yet included in any of other submitted HSE documents and that can be simply linked to the HSE document in the PEP.

Clarification comment and the comment can be closed if Geoview can provide written/verbal reply to above questions. The comment can be closed/addressed in the comment sheet of relevant HSE documents.
comments.response_text:
a) i. Geoview takes proactive measures to minimize the impact of low temperatures on equipment located on the back deck. To prevent freezing and reduce operational downtime, equipment is covered with protective tarps to shield them from water exposure and to mitigate aging caused by environmental factors. Specifically, during winter operations, any equipment on the back deck is thoroughly dried to remove moisture, followed by a layer of insulating fabric to retain heat and an additional protective tarp. This method ensures that the equipment is protected from freezing and helps maintain smooth, uninterrupted operations.

ii. Please refer to Entanglement Procedure_rev01
comments.summary_ko: 저온 위험: 후갑판 결빙 대응 도구/방법론. 엉킴·어업 위험 미언급. Risk register 작성 필요.

A whole Lots (Lot 1, Lot 2 and Lot 3) schedule being prepared and to be finalised with the Lot 1 & Lot 3 VOs.
comments.response_text:
a) It will  be follow to submitted "Appendix 10 Time Schedule (08673169_A)".
Please understand that the data delivery date is decided by completion date of survey, but not start date as many facts such like weather or condition of site.
comments.summary_ko: '조사 시작 후 167일' 대신 '조사 완료 후 N일' 기준으로 납품 일정 정의.

comments.comment_text:
a) Overall, Orsted would like to thank you Geoview for the excellent first round of PEP draft. This has exceeded our expectation and greatly apprecaite the incredible efforts to get it to an amazing level. 
We are very much looking forward to starting operations and successful working relationship.
comments.response_text: a )Being given the opportunity to work on the Orsted PJ is a great honor. We sincerely appreciate your understanding of our shortcomings, and Geoview is fully committed to successfully completing the project in the future.
comments.summary_ko: 전체적으로 우수한 PEP 초안. Orsted 기대치 초과. 감사 표현. (제외 항목)
